entry.title: Re: Mass Upload of Condition Records for Purchasing in S4 Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: Did you find any resolution?

entry.title: Re: Using Ctrl + P on SAP B1 Web Browser
entry.content:
Hi peeraput_nithithanaanan,

Is your question about Web Client for SAP Business One or SAP Business One Browser Access?

For Browser Access, not all shortcuts are supported. Some keyboard shortcuts for SAP Business One conflict with the default browser keyboard shortcuts.

For example, in a Web browser, the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Tab is also for switching tabs.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How can we fetch all the stages of a particular job r...
entry.content: stages such as from which stages the application will go through assessment, offer etc i want that for particular job req

entry.title: Re: Create Button on Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917 ,

oApplication is the object to connect to SAP Business One application (For making UI API connection).

In order to create a Button on System Form (Purchase Order in this case), you first need to make a UI API connection as mentioned in the sample code which I shared in my previous answer.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Screen Personas 400 Session timed out error when clicking on Flavor after upgrade
entry.content:
We recently upgrades Personas to 3.0 SP 19. After the upgrade, system health check is done and notes are up to date.

When I launch a Flavor I see the icons blank and anywhere I click on the flavor, I get 400 Session Timed out error with current timestamp. What am I missing.

Before upgrade screen is

entry.title: Re: Calculating Time Interval between Two Time Feilds
entry.content:
You need to concatenate The Date and Time column to generate a timestamp, then calculate the interval:

Formula of the calculate column "IntervalDays": : DAYS_BETWEEN(TO_TIMESTAMP(CONCAT(START_DATE,START_TIME),'YYYYMMDDHHMISS'),TO_TIMESTAMP(CONCAT(END_DATE,END_TIME),'YYYYMMDDHHMISS'))

entry.title: Recommendations for Implementing Common Localization Requirements for Taiwan with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers various localized features tailored for the Taiwan region, such as financial reporting compliant with local regulations, tax reporting, electronic invoice management, and data compliance processing. For complete information, please refer to Localization for Taiwan in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Over the past few years, many users and implementation partners have provided SAP with valuable feedback following their practical application of the system. This blog focuses on several important topics, drawing insights from SAP solution architects to offer guidance to clients and implementation partners within Taiwan's SAP ecosystem. The goal is to help businesses better utilize the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system to drive digital management.

 

Profit Center Valuation

Profit center valuation is an important part of corporate financial management in some enterprises, focusing on evaluating the financial performance of various business units or departments. By attributing revenue and costs to specific profit centers, businesses can analyze profitability, enhance accountability, and support strategic decision-making. Currently, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support this business requirement. Refer to: 3371144 - Profit Center Valuation is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal. It's recommended for enterprises to understand the current SAP product status and design philosophy during preliminary discussions in the Discover phase and consider adjusting management approaches based on business optimization suggestions from consultants, using SAP's best practices.

 

Withholding and Reporting Tax for Foreign Labor Procurement

In Taiwan, it is common to procure foreign labor to support internal operation and customer sales business needs. Local regulations in Taiwan require companies to withhold and report taxes for such procurements. For details, refer to: Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan. Currently, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not offer a comprehensive localized solution specifically for Taiwan, but alternative application solutions provided by architects can be explored: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax.

If your company or corporate client has similar needs, feel free to log in with your company's S-User account to the following webpage to cast your valuable vote, thereby aiding the SAP product department in better planning the implementation of related functionalities: Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) Management and VAT Reporting in Taiwan

According to Taiwan's relevant regulations, there are currently (as of 2025) five valid forms of GUI. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard end-to-end solution for electronic invoicing. For recommendations on managing GUI within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, application considerations, and implementation suggestions for tax reporting integration with value-added centers, please refer to: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI).

 

LCM Reporting

LCM reports are financial statements based on the IFRS-standard LCNRV (Lower Cost or Net Realizable Value, according to IAS 2 Inventories) inventory valuation method. In Taiwan, companies listed or preparing for an IPO are required to regularly disclose LCM reports. Currently, the inventory valuation standard app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support the LCNRV method, requiring companies to customize reports to fulfill related regulatory requirements. If you need this functionality, please vote on the following page: Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Inventory Aging Report

Taiwan-listed companies need to effectively manage inventory assets using Inventory Aging Reports within ERP systems to ensure compliance and accuracy of financial statements, improve asset liquidity, reduce losses, and enhance competitiveness. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition currently offers a standard application "Inventory KPI Analysis" for enterprises to analyze inventory metrics. Enterprises or SAP implementation partners can also fulfill the need for deeply customized Inventory Aging Reports through custom reporting. More related blogs can be found at  SAP Community .

entry.title: Re: SOST emails sent from which program
entry.content: First of all, did you investigate on the SCOT trace? First try on a DEV or QAS system. If you don't know how to generate an email, ask functional people to help you. If SCOT trace is fine, then discuss with your project/client if running this trace is an option.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Join Condition using a range in Graphical...
entry.content: You can try cross join, add a additional node to filter out useless data. but the performance might be a challenge.

entry.title: Re: BW-MT Transformation lookup rule with compounded objects
entry.content:
The mentioned issue from lechnmic still exists in our system using BW-MT 1.26.15.

Difference to your Scenario @roland_szajko :
Source fields are field-based, only target fields are InfoObjects.

Original Scenario:



After saving and activating the transformation closing + reopening it, first the icon of compounding Object MSOURCE changes.
In original mainteanance it's correctly displayed with the IOBJ icon, now as field based icon even though in datatarget it's an IOBJ.

 

Then aggegation behaviour of MCONVALUE is changed, ->save and activate TRFN.

Suddenly error message appears:

Characteristic MSOURCE compounded to SSALESORG is missing in transformation source

Message no. RSTRAN075

Now, in the lookup rule for 0LOC_CURRCY the source field MSOURCE disappears  and the mapping became inconsistent.

 

Unfortunately SAP wasn't able to provide a fix since September 2024.
Case 990054/2024 - BW4HANA 2021 - inconsistent handling of HANA TRFN Lookup rules in BW-MT 1.25.42 - SAP For Me

entry.title: SAP BAS - BTP deploy failed: throw cds.error `Didn't find auth implementation for ${config}`
entry.content:
Team:

My projects were working at some point. Now, I downloaded from github and cannot be deployed OK. I created a new project and I received this error message: 

 

throw cds.error `Didn't find auth implementation for ${config}`
 
In the past, I created .cdsrc.jos containing:
{
  "requires": {
    "auth": "none"
  }
}
This used to by pass the authorisation checking. Sadly, something has change with security. I modified the MTA.YML and package.json to not use authority checking. The same error message.
 
Any help is welcome.

Tinesh B

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud version 2 blank page
entry.content: Try reading what I posted above. It needs a role. After that you still need to go Settings. It's like starting a new website, there is not magically content there.

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Data Cloud Audit Log for Application
entry.content:
Hi @Rvanjari 

You're right, it's the same audit.search API call, using the same request parameters like userKey, secret, etc. to pull the audit log entries for Application User ( API user). But in that case, the "authType" from the response payload would be "appKey", not "userKey". And you'll also find the name of the CDC application in the "userKeyDetails" field in the response payload. 

Hope that help 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to use the underlying draft enabled enitity seper...
entry.content:
It won't allow you to create on B directly because the composition from A creates a life-cycle dependency. It's like asking a system to create new invoice line items without first telling to which invoice they belong. This makes them orphaned until you 'attach' them to the correct invoice. The solution will be to tell the system from the start which A your new B will belong to. If you don't want to have this tight lifecycle dependency you can switch to normal associations.

To "tell the system from the start which A your new B will belong to" you can do a programmatic creation of the new B (custom action), which will create the new entity, which you can then pop on the screen for the user to complete the details.

To programmatically create new entities, you can use code like the below:

const uuid = generateUUID();
const newB = await srv.create(Entities.B, {
    ID: uuid,
    Parent_ID: a.ID
});

Have a look at the below as well, depending on what your scenario is:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-configure-a-custom-action-that-creates-a-new-object-in-cap-fiori/qaq-p/12520205

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/prefilling-fields-when-creating-a-new-entity-with-cap-node-js/ba-p/13593259

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Dear @smitajalit,

We heard that SAP is going to deprecate basic SF login method for onboardees and force all customers to move to Login to SF via IAS for Onboardees.

I feel this is a huge mistake and was wondering if there any possibility for SAP not to deprecate basic SF login method for onboardees and keep it as an option?

Reasons:

  • The IAS account activation and QR code activation itself involves many steps, such as downloading authenticator, scanning QR code, etc. These steps are thus inherently difficult for users, especially if they are not tech savvy.
  • Onboardees accessing SF prior to day 1 are not even employees at that point. They do not have the luxury or even knowledge or effort in reaching out for support if they may face difficulties in activating IAS and logging in to SF.
    • They may easily give up and hence fail to complete key Onboarding steps such as Personal Data Collection step, posing challenges to HR teams processing their onboarding.
    • Worse, they may even give up joining the Company due to these difficulties
    • In contrast, employees typically have HR, IT, immediate Supervisor, colleagues who can assist them when they face diffculties in activating IAS account 
      • Our experience with customers also tells us that the IAS account activation does indeed pose difficulties to employees, which is why companies have Guides/FAQs/IT support dedicated to make this as painless for employees as possible.
  • I understand that for Employees, there are benefits of IAS in providing SSO to multiple platforms, not just to SuccessFactors. But for Onboardees who has yet to join the company, what multiple platforms are Companies actually giving onboardees access that make the benefits of SSO outweigh the risks caused by the inherent difficulties in account activation and login via IAS?

Overall, I feel there are way more risks than any tangible benefits for Companies to force their Onboardees to use IAS account activation steps to login to SF.

Hi @Gopinath, tagging to share this opinion with you as well. Please re-consider this deprecation lest this makes countless Onboardees' pre-day 1 experiences with the SuccessFactors Onboarding product be tarnished by the difficulties in logging in via IAS in the first place.

Thank you!

Best Regards
Ernest Lin

entry.title: Re: Need to connect to sybase db using sqlanywhere 17
entry.content: SQL Anywhere is a distinct product. You cannot use its client to connect to an ASE database.

entry.title: Re: Optional Filter Variable appears as Mandatory in ODat...
entry.content: It seems only about if you provide a default value for the paramter; if yes, you don't need to explicitly assign a value in your request.

entry.title: Connect to sboGuiApi
entry.content:
Hi

  In below code what value should be passed to sboGuiApi.Connect. Currently it is Dummy value.

sboGuiApi.Connect("0030002");

oCompany = new SAPbobsCOM.Company();
oCompany.Server = "NDB@10.0.1.28:30015";
oCompany.DbServerType = BoDataServerTypes.dst_HANADB;
oCompany.CompanyDB = "LIVE_Database";
oCompany.UserName = "manager";
oCompany.Password = "123";
oCompany.language = BoSuppLangs.ln_English;
oCompany.UseTrusted = true;

int connectionResult = oCompany.Connect();
if (connectionResult != 0)
{
throw new Exception("Error connecting to SAP Business One: " + oCompany.GetLastErrorDescription());
}

SboGuiApi sboGuiApi = new SboGuiApi();
sboGuiApi.Connect("0030002");

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Hi @ernlin Please find my inline response .

@smitajalit  kindly add if i missed any info:

Reasons:

  • The IAS account activation and QR code activation itself involves many steps, such as downloading authenticator, scanning QR code, etc. This steps are thus inherently difficult for users, especially if they are not tech savvy.

Gopi: Onboardee going To receive welcome email from IAS and they just going to set password to activate IAS account. It might be straight forward. i Dont see any difficulty here if IAS has configured properly.

 

  • Onboardees accessing SF prior to day 1 are not even employees at that point. They do not have the luxury or even knowledge or effort in reaching out for support if they may face difficulties in activating IAS and logging in to SF.
    • They may easily give up and hence fail to complete key Onboarding steps such as Personal Data Collection step, posing challenges to HR teams processing their onboarding.
    • Worse, they may even give up joining the Company due to these difficulties
    • In contrast, employees typically have HR, IT, immediate Supervisor, colleagues who can assist then when in activating IAS account 

Gopi :sorry am not understanding why they gonna fail with login .It similar like Gmail login page . Could you please explain bit more on challenges.Only Pre-req you should have IAS setup properly ,then ONB will work seamlessly.

  • I understand the for Employees, there are benefits of IAS in providing SSO to multiple platforms, not just to SuccessFactors. But for Onboardees who has yet to join the company, what multiple platforms are Companies actually giving onboardees access that make the benefits of SSO outweigh the risks caused by the inherent difficulties in account activation and login via IAS?

Like workzone ,they can have user experience of company portal 

Thanks,

Gopi

entry.title: Connection To Database
entry.content:
Hi

 One way to connect to Database is below . One is using Command Line. Which is the best method .

company.Server = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["server"].ToString();
company.CompanyDB = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["companydb"].ToString();
company.DbServerType = SAPbobsCOM.BoDataServerTypes.dst_MSSQL2012;
company.DbUserName = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["dbuser"].ToString();
company.DbPassword = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["dbpassword"].ToString();
company.UserName = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["user"].ToString();
company.Password = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["password"].ToString();
company.language = SAPbobsCOM.BoSuppLangs.ln_English_Gb;
company.UseTrusted = false;
company.LicenseServer = ConfigurationManager.AppSettings["licenseServer"].ToString();

connectionResult = company.Connect();

Thanks

entry.title: Re: IAS + ONB (Welcome Email Day 1)
entry.content:
Yep, that is the workaround in place but not a perfect solution.

The idea is to manage both emails by the same tool at the end, is IAS who is managing the login so the idea is to receive the email from the same place and with the same style.

Maybe a transformation code to detect (as condition) when the User Type changed from Onboardee to Employee, but no idea if exists.

Anyway, thanks for your feedback

Regards.

entry.title: "Help Needed: Identifying Standard CDS View for Sales Order Version Field (VSNMR_V)"
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am currently working on a project in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and need assistance identifying a standard CDS view that includes the version field for sales orders (field name: VSNMR_V).

I have checked the I_SalesOrder CDS view, but it does not seem to include this field. Could anyone guide me on which standard CDS view contains the VSNMR_V field, or suggest an alternative approach to include this field in a custom CDS view?

Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you, [Your Name]

entry.title: Re: Golden transactions in SAP S/4 Professional Services
entry.content:
Hi Payal

Have you considered identifying “Friendly users” and running real transactions e.g. first timesheet, first expense, first project review, first engagement creation with pricing, first report execution with real data. I would try not to create “dummy” and or “unreal” data as this is a production environment and would not want to pollute with “test” data, it can be difficult to reverse or backout these postings, this will also help confirm the authorisations are in place.

Monitor the system and encourage the creation of the first sales invoice, intercompany postings, rev rec, change request etc when you are ready. Don’t rely on the end users identifying issues, be vigilant and check the creation of engagements, related data and the postings thoroughly to ensure they are as per the expected design.

Data migration is always key to ensure the migrated data can now be process as BAU data end to end especially in complex landscapes with multiple interfaces.

Good luck and I hope this helps

Regards

 

Roger

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
CODE MIGRATION

DATA MODEL GENERATION 

UNIT TEST GENERATION

UI GENERATION

entry.title: Crystal Reports for Eclipse & MySQL 8

entry.title: Re: Authentication methods CPI DS Agent
entry.content: Thanks for the reply, As per this link we can use a websocket RFC solution . But when using a websocket RFC connection we will have to maintain the on prem authentication credentials on the cloud . This is the thing we want to avoid and hence searching for another authentication method or another solution like CIDS agent handling the password on prem .

entry.title: Re: PO delivery address
entry.content:
@kaya-ya 

Normally, the delivery address for a purchase order line item is determined based on the following hierarchy:

  1. Storage Location
  2. Plant

If the storage location is not maintained in the purchase order line item, the system will take the delivery address from the plant. The configuration path to maintain addresses for both the plant and storage location is provided in the attachment.

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating a custom AI agent on the BTP
entry.content: In the case some tools I want to use are web services developed using CAP Node.js running on BTP Foundry, I'm supposed to create python wrapper on top or can I use the Node.js version langgraph library. In this second case, is it working via code comment to provide the description of the tools. Is it possible to mix python and node.js tools in one AI Agent ?

entry.title: Re: Technical RFC Integration On-Premise-EWM 9.5 to Cloud...
entry.content:
I'm not familiar with Communication Scenario 0247, but generally you have two options:

  1. Using WebSocket RFC from OnPrem to Cloud - you need to expose the cloud endpoints and they must be reachable from your OnPrem network.
  2. Using classic RFC with Cloud Connector via a Service Channel. 

Are you currently using WebSocket RFC for the other direction (Cloud to OnPrem) or do you use Cloud Connector?

entry.title: Not able to get the workflow instance tasks in my BPA Inbox
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have come across the requirement that, we need to design approval processes that will get triggered from your ABAP env(RAP application). Based on the details, the approver will approve or reject the form.

Above is the basic travel application, the user will create the travel and send it for the approval process. As instance gets created the status for the travel is set to W(Waiting), and the approval form gets triggered.
I'm able to see the task instance in the monitor tab in the BAP but not able to see the task in the inbox of my BPA.

Monitor(Has the triggered task)

Inbox: blank

entry.title: Re: The RBA rules from SAP CDC cannot be disabled
entry.content:
Hi @AndreeaElena 

I tried it on my end using the Status toggle and Delete action, and I can see a similar issue, but if you try a few more times, it will eventually succeed. As you know CDC is running on a huge cluster, sometimes there might be synchronization issues between the nodes.

entry.title: Re: The NEW New Service Connection Process for Energy and Water
entry.content:
Hi,

thank you for this blog. We're really interested into this topic. Currently we have heavy development for this so I hope this will once help us be closer to standard.

From IS-U point, what master data is created from this process? Installation, PoD, premise...?

entry.title: Re: BAPI_CTRACDOCUMENT_TRANSFER in FI-CA module
entry.content:
Yesterday, I faced the same problem and I would like to share the solution with you, although this post is very old.

Call BAPI_CTRACCONTRACTACCOUNT_GOI1 to retrieve the open items. The crucial point: Provide parameter ENQUEUE with X.

After that, call BAPI_CTRACDOCUMENT_TRANSFER and the error message concerning the missing lock will disappear.

entry.title: Helpful assistant for system conversion from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA - ATACA
entry.content:
Motivation:

Customers are now planning a journey for the S/4HANA transformation by converting their SAP ECC systems before being out of maintenance on December 31, 2027. Since this conversion is not simple and is described for each area in a different place, it is time-consuming to search for all relevant information.

Therefor SAP introduced helpful assistant called ATACA - ASU Toolbox Application Conversion Assistant.

In this blog you will find most important information of the assistant and how to use it.

Description:

ASU Toolbox Application Conversion Assistant (ATACA) contains all the information that is not already available in existing SAP S/4 HANA conversion tools, such as guidelines, check tools, migration tools, and SAP Notes to help you convert your system from SAP ERP to SAP S/4 HANA in the most efficient way. ATACA doesn't replace already existing best practices or tools and is SAP application-specific, unlike standard technical conversion guidelines.

ATACA supports the conversion through frequently updated content. This content is a list of execution steps
that are processed within the ATACA during the conversion. As described in SAP Note 3008338, the content
is provided by SAP in the form of an XML file (ASU content).

Benefits:

Conversion costs and time needed are reduced to a minimum.
Avoiding of known bugs during the conversion.
Single point of entry for all kind of information that helps to understand the difference to SAP S/4HANA.

Uploading ASU Content and Creating Task Lists
To generate a task list within ATACA for a system conversion, you must upload the initial ASU content to the
ATACA in the source release, import (1) the content and create (2) task list. Then, you can jump directly into the task list (3).

The tasks in the list are divided to information about the ATACA itselfs and then to 3 main parts -  Preparation, Follow-Up and Customer Conversion Steps.

Each part is split to the Important (mandatory) and Additional (optional) Application Information for each application component. Each application component contains activities/tasks which are recommended to do.

From the task list, you can open particular note on the Marketplace or open SNOTE assistant and implement the note directly.

After processing of the steps mentioned in the note, web page, etc., you can set the status of the task to have overview, how the task ends.

For more detail about the ASU Toolbox Application Conversion Assistant, please see following resources:

  • Central Note ASU Toolbox Application Conversion Assistant 3008338
  • Webinair video
  • Similar blog with very detail description of the tool 
  • Conversion guide
  • Official training opportunity https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-s-4hana-conversion-and-sap-system-upgrade

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
PROCESSGENERATION

Process mapping and identification is most important step. It can also be used for clean core concepts to standardize process and use side by side extension if it doesn't fit into process.Processes needsto be standardized and then based on mapping whether in-app extension or side by side can be decided.

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....
entry.content:
@siyu1, 

If you look in the attachements from Snote 2253571, you will see a document "How to use Instruction Keys in the CGI solution...". This documents discribes how to set up the instruction keys to get the proper service level code in the xml. I hope it helps you!

KR

Gerusa

entry.title: Re: Tutorial / Not able to find 'Define the API' menu path
entry.content:
Hi, this is quite old but today I faced the same issue.

I figured out, that in BTP I needed to add the role collection APIManagement.Selfservice.Administrator to my user. After that the missing activity was present in Integration suite (and after reloading).

For further reference see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configuring-user-access

Best regards, Dirk

entry.title: Re: PO delivery address
entry.content: Thank you : ) !

entry.title: Re: Calculating Time Interval between Two Time Feilds
entry.content: First, you have to involve the Date because start and end date might be different and DSP doesn't support Time type. second, you can replcae the function DAYS_BETWEEN with SECONDS_BETWEEN to get the seconds, then convert the seconds to hours you want

entry.title: Re: Technical RFC Integration On-Premise-EWM 9.5 to Cloud...
entry.content: Hi Simon, thanks for your input. To pick up your points.

entry.title: it_parameter empty in EXECUTE_ACTION method
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In the DPC_EXT class, I have redefined the interface method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~EXECUTE_ACTION, but the import parameter it_parameter is empty, preventing data from being saved or updated.

The strange part is that in the GET_ENTITYSET method, the parameter is correctly filled with the keys.

Has anyone encountered this issue before? Any insights would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP 分析云新功能速递 - 作业监控器
entry.content:
我非常高兴地向大家介绍作业监控器（Job Monitor），它已经随我们2025年第一季度更新版本（QRC1）一起发布！

借助作业监控器，业务用户和管理员将能够更轻松地获取后台作业以及整体流程的状态信息。

 

当前挑战

目前，由于缺乏对多步操作的监控工具，导致多步操作执行过程缺乏透明度。

数据操作、数据导入/导出以及智能预测的监控功能分散，使得用户难以在系统范围内全面监控作业。

并且当用户触发一个多步操作时，他们需要在不同的监控器之间来回切换以查看状态，这使得整个过程变得繁琐。作业状态、持续时间和错误分析分散在各个监控器中，使得用户难以解读整个流程的完整状态。

此外，当用户对某些长时间运行的作业产生疑问，并希望确定其持续时间延长的根本原因（例如，系统繁忙）时，往往很难在任何特定时刻获得作业执行的整体概览。

作业监控器

作业监控器为监控SAP Analytics Cloud（SAC）中的后台作业提供了一个集中的方法。2025年QRC1版本包括了对数据操作作业和多步操作作业的监控功能，未来版本会进一步将更多的作业类型整合到作业监控器中。

借助作业监控器，用户可以轻松监控、排查故障并分析SAP Analytics Cloud（SAC）作业的性能，从而帮助他们简化工作流程，优化数据操作和数据多步操作的配置。

丰富的筛选功能使用户能够从不同角度灵活地搜索作业。例如：

  • 通过“启动日期”筛选器，查看特定时间段内的作业
  • 通过“总持续时间”筛选器，查看长时间运行的作业
  • 通过“状态”筛选器，分析失败的作业
  • 通过“启动依据”筛选器，按日历查看计划作业

作业监控器为作业提供了一个全方位视图，包括作业的触发来源、总执行时间、触发作业的人员以及触发时间等详细信息。在详细页面中，您可以深入了解作业的持续时间、变更记录、参数值等更多内容。

各种的监控页面为不同作业类型和不同作业级别分别提供了定制化的洞察。

  • 为数据操作和多步操作专门设计的监控页面为每种作业类型提供了针对性的洞察。
  • “所有作业”页面为所有数据操作和多步操作作业提供了全面的概览。
  • “主要作业”页面清晰列出了所有一级作业，包括一级数据操作和多步操作作业。嵌套在多步操作或一级数据操作中的作业不会在此页面显示。

高级文本搜索功能支持在作业监控器中进行全局搜索，覆盖作业列表和详细信息页面。借助这一功能，您可以轻松找到任何与作业相关的信息，例如使用了特定维度参数的作业。

支持多种用户角色。

  • 业务用户可以查看他们启动的所有作业、在日历中别人共享给他们的作业，以及他们拥有目标版本的数据操作作业。
  • 管理员或流程所有者可以全面监控所有作业，从而能够全面掌握整个租户的作业状态。

与之前的数据操作监控器相比，作业列表所显现的性能在初始加载和后续分页方面都得到了显著提升。

注意：作业监控器是数据操作监控器的接替产品。

使用作业监控器监控多步操作

作业监控器中的多步操作监控器为多步操作作业提供了完全的透明度和可追溯性。

在专门的多步操作页面上，您可以获取所有多步操作作业的概览。业务用户可以查看他们拥有的所有多步操作作业，而管理员用户可以查看整个租户范围内的所有多步操作作业。

通过选择一个特定的作业，您可以深入了解其详细状态。这包括查看执行进度、分步作业详情、已导入的记录、已变更的记录、参数值等信息。

数据操作步骤的详细状态已集成到多步操作监控器中，用户无需再在不同的监控工具之间来回切换。

对于多步操作中的数据导入步骤，如果有被拒绝的数据，您还可以直接从作业监控器轻松下载被拒绝的数据，而无需切换到数据管理页面。不过，如果您需要了解有关导入作业设置的更多详细信息，仍然可以导航到数据管理页面。

结论

总之，SAP Analytics Cloud 的 2025年QRC1版本引入了作业监控器，这是一个集中式的工具，简化了业务用户和管理员的作业监控工作。它整合了分散的监控功能，提升了透明度和效率。

 

原文链接: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/improved-job-transparency-job-monitor-in-sap-analytics-cloud/ba-p/13966169

原文作者：Sarau Shen

entry.title: Re: Technical RFC Integration On-Premise-EWM 9.5 to Cloud...
entry.content: Point 1) The general guideline for the Integration part on the -Premise system points out to use RFC Connection Type 3 (Connection to ABAP system) - How would I setup the WebSocket RFC within the EWM 9.5 On Premise. What setting is required then on the Public Cloud system to be able to consume the RFC calls?,

entry.title: Re: Create button not visible
entry.content:
Insert the following code in your schema.cds file

@odata.draft.enabled
@odata.draft.bypass
entity entityname {
fields...
...
}


Deploy your code...
Run...

entry.title: Is it possible to hard-code goals in the goal template?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to ask by any chance, if anyone from here experience hard coded the goals for each category in the xml template?

For example, I have financial and customer as categories and then core and consolidated are the goals with specific weight each.

Financial:

  • Core - 10%
  • Consolidated - 10%

    Customer:

    • Communication - 5%
    • Engagement - 10%

       

      And then once I go to the goal section, it is already there and can be use already.

      I just want to confirm if this idea is possible.

      Thank you!

      Best Regards,

      Gelo

      #Performance #Goals #PMGM SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: Technical RFC Integration On-Premise-EWM 9.5 to Cloud...
entry.content:
If connection type 3 should be used, then you need to use option 2).

I don't know which ABAP version EWM 9.5 is based on, and if WebSocket RFC is also available for your specific integration scenario.

entry.title: Re: ABAP ADT parallel locking issue for ABAP Classes
entry.content:
Hi @ThFiedler ,

is there still no way to implement this requirement in ADT? As you write, there were already ideas for this 9 years ago. It is simply totally unfortunate if the whole class is locked in a transport request, even though you only want to edit a single method. For this reason, I work with the SAP GUI again when maintaining classes that are already active. Surely it must be possible to solve this? I will try out @Tamas_Hoznek's suggestion.

Regards Frank

entry.title: Problem Statement: Unable to Deploy CAPM Project on Cloud Foundry – Service Instance Not Found
entry.content:
Dear Exports,

I have developed an SAP CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) application that facilitates salary uploads from Excel to SAP Public Cloud. I followed the official SAP tutorials to develop and deploy my application:

SAP CAPM Introduction Tutorial

Deploy to Cloud Foundry Tutorial

I successfully generated the TESTAPP_1.0.0.mtar file and attempted to deploy it using:

cf deploy /home/user/projects/TESTAPP/mta_archives/TESTAPP_1.0.0.mtar -f

However, the deployment fails with the following error:

Error monitoring creation or update of services: Error creating service "TESTAPP-auth" from offering "xsuaa" and plan "application": Error creating application null (Error parsing xs-security.json data: Inconsistent xs-security.json: Invalid xsappname "TESTAPP-Qorix India Pvt Ltd_qorix-yfvzj2ew_ext-dev": May only include characters 'a'-'z', 'A'-'Z', '0'-'9', '_', '-', '\', and '/'.)

Project Setup Details

  • I have not created any entities under the db folder. Instead, my project consists of:
    • Service definitions (.cds and .js files) under the srv folder
    • Fiori app under the app folder
  • I successfully generated the .mtar file and followed all steps mentioned in the SAP tutorial.
  • Deployment fails during the xsuaa service creation

Actions Performed:

     1. Updated mta.yaml as per the tutorial.
     2. Added the required build script in TESTAPP/APP/Project1/package.json.
     3. Successfully generated the .mtar file.
     4. Attempted deployment via cf deploy, but encountered the above error


Questions:

 

  1. How can I correctly format the xsappname in xs-security.json to avoid invalid characters?
  2. What are the best practices for deploying a CAPM project to CloudFoundry with xsuaa authentication?
  3. What is the best way to handle space-containing organization names in the xsappname field?
  4. Does my project require a db folder with entities for successful deployment, or can I deploy only with services(srv) and a Fiori app(app)?

entry.title: Secure Kafka Integration on SAP Integration Suite: Advantco Kafka Private Link Proxy with Confluent
entry.content:
Introduction

As enterprises adopt real-time, event-driven architectures, Apache Kafka plays a central role in managing high-throughput data streams. Integrating Kafka with SAP Integration Suite opens new possibilities for event-driven business processes—but doing this securely in the cloud can be a challenge.

By combining the Advantco Kafka Adapter for SAP CPI with the Private Link Service on SAP BTP, organizations can securely integrate SAP Integration Suite with Confluent Cloud Kafka clusters, leveraging private networking on AWS and Azure. This approach avoids exposing services over the public internet, meeting strict security and compliance requirements.

 

The Integration Challenge

In traditional setups, Kafka clients—like SAP Integration Suite —must connect directly to Kafka brokers over public endpoints. This connection typically relies on public DNS resolution, meaning the broker’s IP or hostname is exposed on the internet.

For on-premise Kafka installations, customers often configure SAP Cloud Connector to allow SAP CPI tenants to reach Kafka brokers securely. However, this requires additional setup and complexity, especially in hybrid environments.

Confluent Cloud, a fully managed Kafka service, simplifies event streaming. When deployed with Private Networking, it allows private, secure communication between your infrastructure and Confluent Cloud clusters—without public exposure.

 

Enter SAP Private Link Service + Advantco Kafka Adapter

When running Confluent Cloud Kafka clusters with Private Link Service enabled, direct communication between SAP CPI and the Kafka brokers isn’t possible—since CPI tenants can’t reach private endpoints directly.

To overcome this, Advantco provides a Kafka BTP Application, deployed on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, which acts as an intermediary (proxy). It securely forwards requests from the Advantco Kafka Adapter (on SAP Integration Suite) to the Kafka broker via SAP Private Link Service.

Why Private Link Service Matters

Without Private Link:

  • SAP Integration Suite would need to connect over the public internet
  • Potential compliance and security issues arise
  • More complicated networking (VPNs, NATs, etc.)

With Private Link + Advantco BTP App:

  • Private, direct, secure communication
  • No traffic ever leaves the cloud provider’s internal network
  • Simpler, cleaner network architecture

Key Benefits of This Approach

  • Secure Connectivity: No public internet exposure, reducing risk.
  •  Low Latency & High Performance: Direct, private links reduce network hops.
  • Compliance-Friendly: Meets SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR standards.
  • Multi-Cloud & Hybrid Ready: Supports AWS, Azure, and GCP with SAP BTP.
  • Simplified Network Management: No need for VPNs or NAT gateways.

 

Solution Architecture Overview

  1. Advantco Kafka Adapter (SAP Integration Suite)
    • Sends produce requests to Advantco Kafka BTP App
    • Cannot directly access PrivateLink-enabled brokers
  2. Advantco Kafka BTP Application (SAP BTP Cloud Foundry)
    • Acts as the bridge between SAP Integration Suite and Kafka
    • Deployed in a BTP space with SAP Private Link enabled
    • Forwards requests to Kafka brokers securely
  3. SAP Private Link Service
    • Provides private connectivity from SAP BTP to cloud providers
    • Ensures communication with Kafka brokers over private endpoints only
  4. Confluent Cloud Kafka (Private Networking Enabled)
    • Kafka cluster runs in AWS or Azure, accessible via PrivateLink endpoints only
    • No public IP addresses or DNS records are used

Picture 1: Architecture Diagram of the Kafka Private Link Proxy Service on SAP BTP

Flow description:

- Advantco Kafka adapter: an SAP Integration Suite adapter with Private Link Service enabled.

Picture 2: Configure of the Advantco Kafka channel to use Private Link.

- Advantco Kafka Private Link Proxy is an application deployed on BTP Cloud Foundry Space used to receive requests from Advantco Kafka adapter on SAP Integration Suite.

Picture 3: The Advantco Kafka Private Link Proxy application in SAP BTP

- SAP Private Link Service: enables secure, private communication between SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) without exposing traffic to the public internet.

- Confluent Cloud with Private Networking: a private cluster bound with Cloud Provider’s Private Link Service. The cluster can be accessed through Private Endpoint only.

 

AWS and Azure Deployment Scenarios

AWS Deployment

  • Confluent Cloud on AWS provides PrivateLink endpoints in the customer’s AWS VPC
  • SAP BTP on AWS connects via SAP Private Link Service to those PrivateLink endpoints
  • Kafka clients (Advantco Kafka BTP App) connect securely over AWS’s internal network
  • PrivateLink setup requested and managed through Confluent Cloud Console
  • Requires SAP BTP and Kafka broker to be in the same or peered AWS region

Azure Deployment

  • Confluent Cloud on Azure provides Private Endpoints in a VNet
  • SAP BTP on Azure uses SAP Private Link Service to connect to those Private Endpoints
  • Kafka clients (Advantco Kafka BTP App) connect securely via Microsoft’s private network
  • Private Networking enabled and configured via Confluent Cloud Console
  • Requires SAP BTP and Kafka broker to be in the same Azure region

 

Conclusion

By leveraging Advantco Kafka Adapter, SAP Private Link Service, and Confluent Cloud Private Networking, businesses can securely integrate Kafka into their SAP Integration Suite based processes without compromising performance or compliance.

entry.title: Re: Billing doc Creation idoc
entry.content:
If you would like to create an Invoice in SAP system based on details from external system, look up for SAP General Billing Interface. 

This functionality is available both in ECC and S4 HANA. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7b24a64d9d0941bda1afa753263d9e39/0871b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: APIs for me.sap.com
entry.content:
Hello experts,

could you please help me with a question regarding me.sap.com portal... Are there any APIs available, where could I find their definitions? Specifically I'm interested in API which provides functionality around roadmap viewer, like searching for a content there, ...

Many thanks for your support,

Milan

entry.title: SAP B1 Web Client Extension Not Working
entry.content:
We developed an extension as mentioned in the learning hub "Developing Extensions for SAP Business One and the Web Client" and Unit 2 section Creating UI Extensions to Customize SAP Business One, Web Client Views. But when we try to click on the button but no information showing after clicking the button which is developed as mentioned in the learning content. When we do the inspect or troubleshoot that time we found out that its redirect the url to https://<hostname>:5443/svcl/b1s/v2/CompanyService_GetCompanyInfo. But there is nothing mentioned about SVCL redirection i can see other redirection like tcli which is working on SAP B1 web client but i am not able to find any documentation or content regarding svcl redirection for extension.

Regards,

Tushar Nathvani

#SAPBusinessOneWebClient #SAPBusinessOne SAP Business One SAP Business One Extensibility

entry.title: Re: Setting Up the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience: Step-by-Step Configuration and Helpful Tip
entry.content: Not all companies/legal entities have the same enrollment dates for their benefits. Is there any way around this?

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Multistage (Multilevel) intercompany sales and multistage intercompany stock transfer are available on SAP’s external roadmap:
Multistage intercompany sales
Multistage intercompany stock transfer

Current plan is to deliver both with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 (Q1 2026). 
There won’t be any downport option to S/4HANA 2022 or S/4HANA 2023.

The first delivered version of multistage will cover similar scenarios that are covered by advanced, means:

  • Sell-from-stock items are supported
  • Batch split is supported
  • Make-to-Order (with or without variant configuration) is not supported
  • Engineer-to-Order is not supported
  • Sales BOMs are not supported
  • aATP creating subitems is not supported
  • Non-valuated materials / non-stock materials / service materials are not supported

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: Creating 2 Result Analysis version with different RA Method for 2 ledgers
entry.content: Hello Yannick, hello Paulo, did you finally get to a conclusion on this topic, e have the exact same requirement and so far we could not succeed to have a separate posting on dedicated ledger depending on RA Version. Is ti possible or not ? Thank you in advance for your feedback. Regards, Thibaud

entry.title: Do you know SAP Sales Cloud version 2?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 learning journey.

Overview
In this course you will learn about SAP Sales Cloud version 2 from a business user’s perspective. Learn how the solution is used by a sales representative as well as a sales manager in their daily tasks.

Learning objectives

  • Introducing the solution and learning the basics of navigating it
  • Understanding features like guided selling, task management, visit management and more from a Sales Representative's perspective
  • Understanding features like KPI evaluation, forecasting, pipeline management and more from a Sales Managers perspective
  • Exploring Machine learning and Gen AI features like CX AI Toolkit.
  • Understanding features of MS teams integration and relationship Intelligence features
  • Exploring the features of the Mobile Application

Prerequisites

  • Basic Knowledge of CRM Sales Processes

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: APIs for me.sap.com
entry.content: Depending on what exactly you need, you could try this page https://api.sap.com/content-type/API/apis/all

entry.title: BTP useless lock error message
entry.content:
Working on BTP, I get this error message when a colleague is editing an object.

This message is especially useless:

  • the technical username has no identifying information
  • the suggestion to use SM12 on a system where the SAP GUI is not even allowed is rich indeed

Is there a way around it? I mean other than asking in the local chat who is doing what where.

entry.title: Re: Sap certificates renewal
entry.content: Hi  , Do not pay any renewal fee and  subscription . We are professional if you need to sell SAP product  in market ,you need  to support SAP certified professional . Do not pressure on consultant to purchase. Provide free of cost so new professional join SAP AI journey. SAP is now unstable product in market from ECC-->S4HANA--> BTP cloud . Do you think Industry ready to invest blindly in SAP . SAP Professional every year pay alot time and money in certification . It's  a sign of unstable product that release update every year. Microsoft Power app replace SAP fiori app create front end and we can use microsoft data base and service then what is the need of SAP in market

entry.title: Re: Setting Up the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience: Step-by-Step Configuration and Helpful Tip
entry.content: Hi @Patricia88 You can create one template for each legal entity/company if dates are different.

entry.title: Re: CO10 : CO684 - You cannot make an assignment to make-...
entry.content:
Did you already perform the checks of 1929029 - FAQ: Settlement rules in production order for your case?

  • Check whether the settlement profile permits settlement to the required recipient.
  • There must only be one mandatory recipient in the settlement profile.
  • Check the material type of the header material. The system can only create a settlement rule automatically if the material is managed with values (see Customizing transaction OMS2).

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Join Condition using a range in Graphical...
SAP Build Process Automation - ProcessAutomationDelegate explained (Security and Roles too)
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Couple of months ago we introduced the ProcessAutomationDelegate. Let's find out what it brings for Governance among other Role Collections.

Typical Tasks

While setting your tenant, questions can quickly come for knowing WHO is the admin and WHO is not. Admin can do EVERYTHING within SAP Build Process Automation. Here an overview of the different tasks assigned by user profile with the corresponding Role Collections:

ProcessAutomationDelegate?

In a nutshell, ProcessAutomationDelegate is like a ProcessAutomationDeveloper without the right adding a project or add anything without being granted in a container such as Project or Environment. This role collection is convenient when:

  • you need to assign a user to monitor instances and jobs execution
  • you need to assign a user to a specific project without giving the opportunity to create a new project
  • you need to assign a user to a specific environment without giving too much responsibility

Combined with Shared Environment Privileges, you can customize your governance rules.

  • More information about Role and Role Collections for SAP Build Process Automation is available here.
  • More information about Shared Environment rights is available here.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Transition guidelines from Public Environment to Shared Environments
entry.content:
Dear Community,

In a previous blog post "How to transition to Shared Environments - getting started" we provided a getting started on how to transition from Public Environment to Shared Environment. Let's discover the "transition guidelines":

Is the project currently deployed on public environment?

  • If the project is not currently deployed on a public environment, you can simply create a shared environment and deploy.
  • If the project is currently deployed on a public environment and you don't need to keep the past instances (typically in a development tenant) you should consider:
    • Undeploy on public environment
    • Deploy on a shared environment and test the outcome
  • If the project is currently deployed on a public environment and you need to keep the past instances (typically in a Test or Production tenant) you should consider:
    • Deploy on a shared environment and test the outcome first
    • Stop using on public environment

Are you using "API Trigger" (*)?

Let's see the difference between a Process/Workflow deployed within a Public Environment compared to a Shared Environment:

  • When a process is deployed within the Public Environment, you can click on "Start Instance" to test your workflow, and quickly after the process is started
  • When the SAME process is deployed within a Shared Environment, clicking on "Start Instance" won't be possible and you'll end up with an error.

How to fix (**)...

  1. Open the corresponding process
  2. Add a trigger
  3. Select API Trigger
  4. Provide a name
  5. Your process contains now an API Trigger

How to test...

You can now click the "start instance"

How to call an API Trigger with Environment?

Calling an API with an environment means an extra parameter:

Example with an Action Project

Using your favorite API development tool, you'll need:

  • Add the environment parameter
  • Add an API Key
  • Test!

Development Tenant VS. Production Tenant

It's a good practice to create on both tenants the same environment id (identifier). The only difference between the two tenants would be the API Keys.

More information about:

  • API Keys - help topic
  • Processes and Workflows within Environment - help topic
  • Manage Destinations - help topic
  • Triggers - help topic
  • Add an API trigger to a project - help topic

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Pricing Condition Tables in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @AndreeBT,

could you please check if CDS View I_PurgPrcgCndnRecdValidity would fulfill your requirement?

 

best regards,

Marius

entry.title: how get email body for billing document all configurations have been done but no body
entry.content:
Hi ,

We have mantained the configuration for body of email for billing documents , we have used custom output type in NACE we have set everything correct .I also checked SBCOMS_SEND_REQUEST_CREATE 

and created an implict enhancement but it does not work  the break point doesnt get there . Any other suggestions on how to make it work using ABAP .

 

 

Best regards,

Pradeep.

entry.title: Re: how to deploy databricks jdbc drivers in SAP Cloud In...
entry.content:
Dear Varun,

I suggest you ask this question to support team/component LOD-ANA-AQU.
You can raise a support case as explained in following SAP note.

1296527 - How to create a support case for SAP Support - SAP for Me

Regards,
Riccardo

entry.title: Inventory Posting of stock count items row limits
entry.content:
Hi All

I would like to ask if there is an increase in row limits for Inventory Posting of stock count items in SAP version 10 as the previous version 9 limits the rows to 999?

 

 

Kedalene Chong
